# PASTA Risk Checklist: Guided PASTA Risk Analysis

**Purpose**: Validate whether the guided PASTA risk specification is complete, clear, consistent, and review-ready across workflow guidance, publish-to-risk behavior, and reporting.
**Created**: 2026-06-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the stage-specific guidance requirements explicit about what information the user must supply at each of the seven PASTA stages, not just that guidance exists? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the spec define what counts as an acceptable "overall risk outcome" narrative for stage-seven publish readiness? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011A]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements defined for how many distinct stage-seven risk conclusions a single PASTA model may contain and how they remain distinguishable in review and publication flows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Spec §User Story 2]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the spec fully define the data that must be carried forward from earlier stages into Risk and Impact Analysis, rather than describing it only at a high level? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK005 Are the minimum publication-ready fields complete for both first publication and republish scenarios, including any required notes or source-link metadata? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-011A, Spec §FR-013]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "more guided" translated into observable requirement language, such as concrete prompt, context, progress, or missing-information behaviors, rather than left as subjective wording? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "same scoring scale and interpretation" specific enough to avoid ambiguity about whether PASTA must match only numeric values or also severity labels, thresholds, and wording from the existing STRIDE-style workflow? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "same style of the application" defined clearly enough for reviewers to distinguish required reporting consistency from optional visual polish? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the distinction between a published risk-workspace "projection" and the PASTA source record stated clearly enough to avoid conflicting implementation interpretations? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §FR-013B]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the spec clear about what "active revalidation flag" means from a reviewer perspective, including where that state is visible in the UI and reporting surfaces? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-019]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the publication rules remain consistent across user stories, functional requirements, edge cases, and assumptions with no contradiction between explicit publishing and synchronization language? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK012 Are the authorization requirements consistent between the clarification that editors may publish, the formal authorization impact text, and the existing threat-module role model referenced elsewhere? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-020, Spec §FR-020A, Spec §Authorization Impact]
- [ ] CHK013 Do reporting requirements stay consistent about the supported v1 surfaces everywhere they are mentioned, including stories, requirements, success criteria, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-017, Spec §SC-005A]
- [ ] CHK014 Are the source-of-truth requirements consistent with the edge-case language about edits in the risk workspace, so there is no silent shift of authority after publication? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §FR-013B]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Can reviewers objectively determine when stage-seven scoring is complete and publishable based on the acceptance scenarios and functional requirements alone? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-011A]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the success criteria for reporting consistency measurable enough to judge whether outputs actually match the application's established style? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005, Spec §SC-005A]
- [ ] CHK017 Is the success criterion for reaching an initial scored stage-seven conclusion in under 25 minutes sufficiently bounded by scenario assumptions to be repeatable across reviewers? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK018 Can the spec's no-duplicate requirement for republishing be objectively verified from the written requirements without inferring hidden implementation details? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-013, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 Are primary, alternate, and recovery scenarios all covered for the publish lifecycle: first publish, blocked publish, republish after edits, and review of an already published conclusion? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK020 Does the spec cover how reviewers should interpret partially complete PASTA models that contain scored but unpublished conclusions, published but revalidation-blocked conclusions, or no publishable conclusions at all? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK021 Are cross-surface review scenarios covered sufficiently for both threat-module reviewers and risk-workspace consumers, or is one audience underspecified? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-018]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK022 Does the spec define what should happen if a linked risk-workspace record is closed, changed independently, or becomes inconsistent with the source PASTA conclusion? [Gap, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §FR-013B]
- [ ] CHK023 Are failure-mode requirements defined for publication attempts when the risk workspace is unavailable, the linked risk record cannot be refreshed, or publication partially succeeds? [Gap, Exception Flow]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the spec cover whether multiple PASTA conclusions may intentionally publish to separate risk records for the same business area without being mistaken for duplicates? [Gap, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-004]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK025 Are audit requirements specific enough to define which score changes, publication actions, blocked publishes, and republish events must be attributable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021, Spec §Audit/Event Impact]
- [ ] CHK026 Are localization requirements complete for new stage-seven scoring, publish-state, and risk-link terminology across both interactive and exported surfaces? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022, Spec §Localization Impact]
- [ ] CHK027 Are responsiveness expectations defined clearly enough for publish and export flows, or do they rely on unstated assumptions about synchronous behavior? [Gap, Spec §SC-002, Spec §Operations Impact]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK028 Are the assumptions about the existing STRIDE-style scoring model and risk workspace capabilities documented strongly enough to expose what happens if those shared semantics change later? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK029 Does the spec clearly identify which existing threat and risk behaviors are reused versus newly introduced, so reviewers can separate true dependencies from desired outcomes? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-015, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK030 Is there any unresolved ambiguity about whether publication is allowed only from current stage-seven conclusions or also from older published conclusions that are now informationally stale but still visible? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011A, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK031 Do the requirements avoid conflating "publication", "synchronization", and "projection", or do those terms still need a glossary-level clarification for implementers and reviewers? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-013A, Spec §FR-021]

## Notes

- Balanced coverage requested: guided workflow, publish-to-risk integration, and reporting/export consistency.
- Audience assumption: PR reviewer.
- Depth assumption: Standard.
